name: Irish Cream & Chocolate Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine wafers and butter in a bowl. Press into a 13 x 9 inch glass baking dish. Refrigerate.
2. In a saucepan, cook gelatin, salt, coffee, and milk over medium heat until hot and coffee is dissolved. Reduce heat. Stir a little of the coffee mixture into egg yolks, then pour egg mixture back into saucepan.
3. Cook, stirring frequently, until the mixture thickens slightly (4 to 5 minutes). Remove from heat and add liqueur.
4. Using an electric mixer or food processor, blend cream cheese and 1/4 cup sugar until smooth. Slowly blend in the coffee mixture. Chill, stirring occasionally, until the consistency of raw egg whites, about 1 to 1-1/2 hours.
5. Meanwhile, melt chocolate in a double boiler over hot water. Let cool. Beat egg whites until soft peaks form. Gradually add remaining sugar and beat until stiff peaks form.
6. Gently fold egg whites into the coffee mixture. Transfer 1-1/2 cups of the mixture to a small bowl and fold in chocolate.
7. Using a large spoon, alternately spoon the two mixtures over the chocolate crust. Use a knife to swirl the chocolate through the coffee mixture. Refrigerate until firm, at least 6 hours.
